Hey everyone, this is Russell again and today I'm really excited to share with you guys an interview with one of the people that I originally learned public speaking from. He was the person who got me excited about going on this journey and learning this art that has literally made me tens of millions of dollars and I promise you as you get deeper and deeper into this you are going to just love it. And a couple cool things. Number one, his name is John Shielders and I'll tell the story deeper in the interview. I'm not going to go too deep into it now but he started speaking on stage when I was like two years old, like very beginning of this whole thing and a lot of the great stage presenters came through John's speaker training and they learned from him. And as you'll see in the interview he used to charge $25,000 for speaker training but John has been retired since 2010 and literally since then it's kind of the industry has kind of died. I think he was the one who was training and teaching and coaching people through that and then he just kind of stepped away from the training of it and since then really good speakers, there's not a lot of them anymore. And so I was so excited to have him first off to agree to do an interview, to come out of retirement to share this stuff with you. And then after we got off the call I asked him a question and said, hey John, everything you ever taught the most powerful thing are your children's chunks and you have a whole entire course that you used to sell for $25,000 and went through this whole process. Is there any way I can license it from you to share with everyone who's going through this training right now? And luckily for me and for you he said yes, we actually spent a little fortune licensing the entire thing from him and so after you watch the interview down below here in the membership site, you'll be able to actually go and watch the actual children's chunks. Now this gets me excited because I bought these way back in the day. I have them in the audio book and I listen to them on my headphones all the time because I learned the way he tells his stories, you can learn them, you can model them, you can match them and start plugging them into your presentations. So just kind of preset up what's going to be, you're going to learn on this presentation, the interview and then also using going through all the children's chunks. When you start writing your presentation, basically you have this timeline, right? And something I learned from John initially. You have this timeline of your presentation, right? If you're going through the presentation, you're teaching, you're telling stories, you have a good time and all of a sudden back here is where you come and you start asking somebody for money. If you do that, all of a sudden it's like hitting a brick wall and tons of people are going to be like, oh, all of a sudden, no, you're asking for money and all these objections start popping up and it gets really, really hard. And so what I learned from John, this concept he calls children's chunks, is you figure like what are all the concerns that somebody's going to have when they get to this spot, right? They're going to have a concern about this and a concern about this and a concern about like all these concerns are potentially going to come up. So what you do then is look at each of these concerns and you take each of those and basically wrap it in a story. Now I call them stories, he calls them children's chunks, but it's a story, right? A story that's going to resolve that concern. And then you take that story and you plug it in somewhere in your timeline. So say, okay, I'm going to tell a story right here that's going to knock down that concern. Then I'm going to tell a story right here that knocked down that concern. I'm going to tell a story here until you're plugging in these chunks, these stories, all throughout your presentation. And then what happens now is you're doing a presentation, you're going through this, you're telling a story after a story and the people are laughing, they're engaging, all these things are happening. By the time they get to this spot right here, we're asking for money. All their objections have already been crushed. And by the time they get there, it's like, oh, sure, I'll give you money. It makes the selling process so much simpler, so much easier. So in the interview, John's going to talk about how he kind of invented this and discovered it and how it works and how you kind of tie all these things together. In fact, during the presentation, he shares two or three of his children's chunks with you in kind of, you know, in some detail. But then like I said, we license the course so you can go through and listen to all the children's chunks. And so now his stories, they may not be your stories, but a lot of the stories you can just tell like the story about Napoleon Hill, you can take that story because it's going to resolve the same concern that anyone has about buying anything. You plug that story in, there's different stories. And so I recommend after you go through this interview, you understand what children's chunks are and how they work is then go through the children's chunks training, listen to the children's chunks, memorize and learn them and then you can start plugging those things into your presentation to the timeline. So that's kind of what this presentation is. The interview and the entire course down below, you are going to love it. Again, in the past people paid $25,000 to learn this information you guys have right now inside this training. I hope you love it. I hope you use it. I hope it helps you to get all of your objections answered before we ever get to the sell because it makes your selling so much easier when you do that. Without said, thanks again, you guys appreciate you. And we're going to dive into the interview with John Childers right now. Hey, everyone, it's Russell Brunson. We're welcoming you back to the program. I am so excited for today because I've got somebody with me who is someone I've looked up to now for over a decade. In fact, he is the person that got me started on my speaking career. He probably doesn't even know this, but I went to my very first internet marketing seminar. It was one of Arman's seminars and I didn't know anything about what was going to happen. I showed up at the first event. I had my laptop there like a good internet nerd and I was going to be taking notes and I saw the first person get on stage and speak and he sold something. I remember watching all the people run at the back and I remember kind of being confused but then doing the math. I even saw a 2,000 hour product. I was doing the math too, four or six. That guy just made like $60,000 an hour and next I got up and he did his thing. For three days I'm watching this and then the very last speaker was you and you got on stage and you said, do you guys notice what's been happening over the last three days? I'm this like 22 year old kid. I'm like, yes, I have been noticing exactly what happened. Then you started talking about this public speaking business which I didn't even know was a thing. Then I watched you and I watched, I watched you do your art, which was one of the most fascinating things. In fact, there's two or three things that I noticed you do that very first time. I still do my presentations, which is kind of fun. People are like, where did you come up with that? I'm like, there's this guy named John Childers. I heard a decade ago and he was the best I'd ever seen. As we're kind of putting together this program, I've gone back to like who are the people I learned all these original things from and that's why it's such an honor for me to be here with you. Thank you man for being part of this training. I'm so excited to have you here. My honor. So cool. So you've been retired though since, so 2010 is when you kind of retired from the whole speaking business and now you're just hanging out having a good time sounds like. That's true. You know, I was forced into retirement because my wife got cancer and I couldn't be traveling all over the world with her going through the chemo and all that, but she's in remission now. But meanwhile, I have learned that there's something I'm really, really good at and that's doing nothing. So retire about pitch me well. That is so cool. Now I want to kind of just tell one more story and then I'm going to start asking you questions, but it's interesting. So 2010 is when you retired. What's interesting is in the speaking world where people are going on stages and selling about that same period of time as when, at least from my standpoint is when events started becoming more and more scarce. I think the economy shifted and less events started happening and there was a gap there for a couple of years where like there weren't very many events. And I feel like all of the legends, all the people that you had trained and all the people kind of learned from you originally, like a lot of them just kind of disappeared. And I was lucky enough, I had the chance to learn from you and from some amazing speakers back in the day. And then there was this gap and it felt like everyone who was really good at speaking from the stage kind of retired. And then fast forward four or five years later when webinars started coming out and events start happening, I feel like I had an unfair advantage. I started coming in. I was the only one that knew these skills that knew these things. I started going to stages and I don't think I was ever an above average speaker, but I was able to come and just out close everybody because I knew the things that you had pioneered so many years ago. And I always tell people, oh my God, I feel like what you do, what I do now is like such a dead art and I'm trying to bring it back. Cause like, man, I don't know if I told you, but I spoke to the event the other day or a little bit ago and we did a $3.2 million in sales in 90 minutes and people were freaking out. I was like, I only closed like 9.5% of the room. I was like, I was actually disappointed. I didn't do more because I know what should have happened. You know what I mean? But, but it's such a dead art. And so I'm so excited to have you come and impact this next generation of people who are trying to figure this stuff out because now there's so many events happening and there's so many online platforms to speak at. But it's just, it's an interesting thing. I'm curious like when did, when did you kind of jump in this? When did you start pioneering it back in the day? Oh my goodness. Russell, I did my first seminar. In 1982, I was celebrating my 20th anniversary of joining the Air Force and instead of retiring from the Air Force, I decided to retire from real estate investing. And so it was a big event for me. And I remember I, I charged $100 and I had 131 people there. So I'm sitting there. I made $13,000 for doing a three day event. This is great. I have got to learn more about this. But I started then and the seminar business was new. Selling information from state, we were like the pioneers of that industry. But I didn't really get good until 1990, eight years later. And I had a situation where a friend was down on his luck and I called him up and gave him an opportunity. He was an awesome closer. And I don't want to go into depth on this because it's a long story, but he said, I will train one person how to do my skill. And that's John Childers. And man, I studied it inside and out. And I had never learned the psychology behind a presentation until he shared it with me. And then I decided that I would share it with two people. And that's my two sons, JJ Childers, who is now a seminar speaker on asset protection, Tracy Childers, who has a software company and he's big on the internet. My son said, Dad, we're using it, share it with others and start teaching these skills. And when I started teaching my million dollar speaker training, I was blown away. I had no idea that people in the general public would like to learn how to be a speaker and how to close from the platform. It's been a great career. And Russell, it's a lack of recipe. I used to call it a shell. And I'll hold my fingers up and I said, if you learn, you put this, this, this, this, this in your presentation. Now, you can take your content and fill in between the pieces. And because you have this shell, you will sell. That's what the Childers Chunks all about is because I teach a chunk that they could memorize and they can adapt it to their presentation. So the Childers Chunks, by the way, if those are watching, this is the DVD course I still have to this day, which is the Childers Chunks, which I have studied like crazy. And do you want to explain kind of what a Childers Chunk is so that those who aren't as familiar as I am with it? I will. And now, as you hold those up, it has my picture across the spine of, I think there's six volumes there. Yeah. Now, those were $195 per volume. And if you didn't buy them all, you didn't get the whole picture. You missed the nose. That was kind of like the psychology behind it. But in the Chunks is I started by teaching people that there's a timeline of every presentation. Now, your presentation can be 15 minutes, 90 minutes, which is the typical what you and I would do from stage might be 90 minutes. But your presentation could also be three days long, like an entire training. Here it is, you need to start with a timeline and know what you want to accomplish in the timeline so that your audience is thinking what you want them to think at a certain time. If you would start with the presentation design, it starts with the end in mind. What are you going to want to happen at the end of your presentation? Now, you may think, well, I want to sell something. Well, that's more definitive to that. Let's go through what you really need to know about the end of your presentation. Are you number one going to sell a product or are you going to ask them to give you their email address and sign up for whatever or do you want them to call a telephone number? That's part of the end that you have in mind. All of this has to do with what you're going to be teaching in the presentation. So the end in mind, I want them to get up and run to the back of the room to give me $5,000. That was in my mind. The first time I ever did that was for a $5,000 real estate training. I give the same presentation today if I want them to get up and run to the back of the room to give me $5,000 toward their speaker training program. When you start with the end in mind, now you can put up here, what are the obstacles that someone would go through before they would get up and run? Number one, that requires action. So one of my children's chunks is about educating your audience to take action. It has nothing to do, they don't know what's coming, but I have to educate them about taking action. Nothing happens until you make a decision. And I need to teach something in the timeline about making a decision. If I want them to pull their wallet out and put a credit card back there, I have to teach something that has to do that. I know that people have a tendency to, well, let me think about that. So I have a chunk that addresses that issue way before it ever comes up. And then as you might remember, whatever my audience is they're listening, they have what's called an aha moment because I teach with what's called the immersion method of learning. Nothing makes sense until the end. And so I can teach way more material by telling them that logic is the slowest component of their brain. So I'm not going to teach in a logical manner. I want you to go fast. I want you to learn this. I want you to be able to make changes immediately in your life. And if that's okay, I'm going to use the immersion method. That allows me to take the different chunks and teach whatever I want and I'm plugging it in and they love it. Then at the end of my presentation, when I want them to take action, all of a sudden, all these things that I've taught come together because my closing request requires them to go through this process that I have taught all through here. That was a long answer. Oh, I know. I love it. Because I remember after the first time I heard you speak, I got invited to speak somewhere. So my head, I was like, oh, I know what to do. And so before going through any of your training or anybody's training, I go to this event. I stand on stage and my thought process was I think a lot of people think through initially is like, I'm just going to teach the best stuff I have and then they're going to be obsessed with it and then make an offer. They're going to have to buy it. And so I did that. And I remember I'd made my offer at the end and nobody moved and it was so embarrassing. And then I started learning from you. And you call them children chunks, but there's these stories you're telling throughout. And the whole presentation, it's not just like, teach them a bunch of stuff in the end, you're going to ask for sales. You're crafting this whole timeline, this whole presentation for that. For example, I know one of the biggest concerns I get all the time, people ask me is like, well, Russell, this is so good. Why don't you give it to me for free? And so I always get that thing and then I'll pay you from the profits. I'm like, oh, there's a story that I have, like a Russell chunk, I guess I could call it. My wrestling coach in college, I remember my second day I was wrestling. He came in, he came and knocked on my door and I opened the door and he walked in and he was like, hey, Russell, I want to give you this DVD. This is all my matches. I want you to watch these things. I'm like, okay, cool. I take the, or it was the VHS at the time. I took the VHS from him. He said, wait, before I give this to you, I need you to go get your wallet. I was like, I was kind of nervous, but my coach was like a UFC fighter and like very intimidating person. I grabbed my wallet, handed to him and he opens it up and he took all the cash out of my pocket out of my wallet and put it in my pocket, his pocket and he handed it back to an empty wallet. I was like, coach, why, why did you just take all my money? And he said, I'm going to teach you a lesson that will serve you for the rest of your life. He's like, if you, if I gave you that DVD for free, you would take it, you put it on your shelf and you wouldn't do anything with it. But because you've invested it now, he's like, now you're going to take it seriously. You're actually going to take actions. I don't want to deprive you from, from actually having success because of this. So that's why I'm charging you. So I tell a straight front. So by the time at the end, nobody ever asked me again, like, well, if this is so good, why don't you just get to me for free and I'll pay you when I make money from it. Because like I've addressed that earlier in the presentation through a really fun story. And like that's kind of stuff you're talking about is telling those stories throughout that resolve all the concerns. So by the end they, there's no more concerns left. They just have to give you money. Well, let me give a tip to your audience that put me over the hump into the top for many years. I was the top selling speaker out there and there's several reasons for that. I'll share them with you right now. Number one, my price was so high. So if your price is really high, it doesn't take that many sales to have the highest grossing sales. But your story made me think about something that changed my life forever. When people said, let me pay you out of the profits. This is right out of my presentation that I would do. I get a person, I said, how many of you believe I'm going to try to sell you something? Well, you're not going to be disappointed. Fact is, could I take 60 seconds and make my sales presentation now? And let's just get it over with. And so you're asking permission to sell something in your audience. And she says, yeah. So I said, OK, I have what has been labeled the world's most expensive training. It's $50,000. And I had people say, 50. Are you out of your mind? Wait a minute. You have to understand. Does it make sense that you would, if you could make $250,000 is $50,000 to learn or do that a very small amount? And somebody says, well, I'll tell you what, if you're that confident, why don't you let me just come to your training, learn these inside secrets, and then I'll pay you out of what I make with what I learned from you. And I said, you know, that's exactly how I do my training. And I watched the audience. What? Yes. You make a commitment enrollment fee and I will finance your entire training in which, let me ask you another way. How many of you will give me a dollar if I give you $4? No, no, no. Before you answer that, let me ask you in a different way. How many of you would give me a dollar if I give you $4? However, you don't give me my dollar until I have given you your $4 and every hand in the room will go up. And that's a chunk. This in my presentation. That's the fact is I used to teach real estate investing. So I want you to picture that. Maybe you know how to invest and maybe you don't. But let's just look at a great real estate investment. It's $50,000 for a rental property. It's 20% down payment, but seller financing for the rest of it. Who, by the way, there's no interest on this loan. And guess what? If it rents for four times your payment and for some reason you don't rent it that month, you don't have a payment that month. How many of you would love an investment like that? And I get their hands in there. Well, that's exactly what my training is. It's that recipe. Exactly. You're going to put up a down payment and I'm going to share all the insider secrets. And when you use in them, if it makes money, you send me my dollar. If it doesn't make, then you don't have a payment. By how many of you would like to get into a program like that? Okay, now I'm teaching in my timeline, I'm getting down toward them. Because that happens, because I taught that section in my presentation at the end, they're going to know what to do. So they have to fill it out. Now they know that people saw them put their hand in there. So they have to stand up. So getting them on their feet in their presentation is critical to the buying process because now they have taken action. Okay. Now that you're on your feet, headed in the right direction. I want you to come down front and join me on stage because that's where you'll get your speaking. They come on, come on down front. Now at that time, I had never, ever seen a speaker get people down on the stage. I today, a lot of people copied that step. But when I watched them, I can tell they didn't go through my chunks because I do this constantly time out. Why did I do what I just did? And if they understood the psychology, it would work more effectively for them. So when I would get people down front, I would tell them, okay, I'm going to use about three minutes of your time at 300 dollars. Let's just round it up to $1,000. I want everybody to take your enrollment form and I want you to mark through your down payment and reduce it by $1,000. So that you will get your speaking fee immediately tonight. It makes you have a pry, you have a feeling you just don't get anything else. They're feeling that. Now, when it comes to, I want these people, I'm not trying to sell them into a program. I'm trying to show them and let them experience what my life and Russell, what your life is like, what's it like to be a speaker? What's it like to have information that you can share with people that changes their life positively, that lets people in your audience immediately go out and put together something that they learn and make money with it? When you have that skill, you ought to use it. That was a long answer. I love it. I'm totally geeking out on this. I love it. So cool. Man. So cool. I was, I was, I was this morning as I was preparing for this call and thinking about it. There's one thing I remember that I saw you do the very first time I saw you speak. It had such an impression on me. I'd use it today a lot, but it's, it's, I remember you had everyone had out the forms and they were filling them out. And you kind of start playing with the audience a little bit. And I think you, you're so good at this. Um, and I don't see people do it nowadays, but I kind of try to do, I get more nervous. I'm not super comfortable doing it, but I remember you were, you're telling people like, fill out the form, like, go fill that right now. And then, and then you're asking me, like, like, why aren't I filling it out? And someone said something. And, and I remember you're like, if it, if it's your wedding, get your phone, let's call your wife, let's call your fiance right now and let's let her know this is like, you can get married any day of the year. This is the only day my speaker training is happening. You need to like cancel your wedding because this is more important. You need to marry the next weekend. And you can't go through all these excuses and it's like having fun with the audience and everyone's laughing and like is, it's just some, I don't know, it's level of mastery when you can start playing with the audience and having fun with that. I think some people get nervous and they get there and they're, you know, they got their slides and they're, and they're protected, but you would get in the audience, just play with them. And I'm curious, like, we always that way, or was there a certain level of comfort or when did things like that start happening? I don't know. I, I feel that God gave me a gift. I am just, I don't change in front of the room. I'm just, I have fun. I enjoy it. And that's just a natural ability I have, I guess. But if we have, can I teach one more chunk that I think would have value to your people up there? Like, yeah. Have you ever taught real estate investing? No, I never have. Well, the way speakers typically make money is this, you do something successfully and then you teach people how you did it. So I made my first million dollars in real estate in just over three years. And then I made millions teaching people how I made my million. Well, in real estate, something happens there that if you don't get this piece, you will not be successful as a real estate. This is an insider secret. People used to come and ask me, John, you were, there were like 300 people in that real estate seminar and three of you are now millionaires. Why were you able to do it? And these other people didn't. Now, Russell, I know people ask you that constantly. Well, I did everything I was supposed to do and I just can't seem to make any money in the internet business or the speaking business or the real estate business or whatever. I know that's the case, but I, I know now what the secret is. It has to do with taking action. So I learned something that I want everybody, if this was a live audience where I was watching them, I wouldn't make them do it. But I want everybody to pull out a clean piece of paper and drop down at the end. Three lines from the bottom. Now let's say five lines from the bottom. I want you to write this. I'll take it unless I change my mind. Now let me say that verb. I'll take it. Unless I change my mind. Now I want you to sign it. And you know what, Russell, everybody in the audience would sign that piece of paper and as soon as they signed it, I said, okay, why were you willing to sign that piece of paper? Why were you willing to take action, take the one action that's required to do something? Why were you able to do that now? And yet you won't go out into the real world and take an action like that. They said, well, if I have the right to change my mind, I don't want to sign it again. Exactly. Now, if you put that sentence on a real estate contract, you will not get one accepted, but what could you say that means the same thing? What if you put this secret language, secret to us? This contract is subject to getting adequate financing acceptable to buyer. What does that really mean? It means I'll take it unless I change my mind. What if you had this sentence in there? This contract is subject to my partner's approval. What does that mean? It means I'll take it unless I change my mind. But those of you who are watching, what you have just learned is what action, what inside secret can I learn that will allow me to take the critical action that's necessary to be successful as real estate agent? Now, you may have an opportunity someday to see a tremendously profitable opportunity, but what could be in that contract that would make you sign it immediately? As speakers, we should call attention to our contract in which we have a 100% money back guarantee because I want everyone in my audience to understand that I need you to sign that contract today. I need you to take decisive action today. However, you have the right to change your mind and that should eliminate because if you cannot take that action, you won't get a scholarship when opportunity comes in 13 minutes. You won't get the speaking fee that comes quicker than that. You will not be able to get any of the rewards, any of the profits that go to people who have learned to take action unless you have learned how to take decisive action, sign your name and make that offer. Now, if your people put that in your presentation somewhere, you're teaching good, valuable information, but then when it gets down to the part of the presentation in which they're going to ask for an order, ask them to do, these people say, aha, aha, because it all starts making sense at the one critical moment that people have to make that decision. To each, teaches a valuable piece of contents. They give that a high five. Awesome. That's up everything. So at the end, all of those, all of their, their concerns or whatever's keeping from buying it, like all the things have fallen down. So they just slide right, slide right through it. It's fascinating. How, and a couple of questions, follow up questions. So one of them is easily show their chunks, which is really interesting. Is it, is that you can use it in a real estate presentation, use it in public speaking presentation, in an internet marketing presentation, probably in a weight loss presentation, like, and I'm assuming over the years, all the speakers you train, you probably have people in most every market and they work in all of them, right? Yes. You know, you got to three major markets that people choose something from, uh, you know, relationships or, uh, healthcare or wealth. I was in the wealth arena and, and because that's where I was, my audience was normally in that. So we were monopolized by wealth people and wealth speakers, but it works anywhere, anytime. I took the same chunks and I had somebody call me up and say, John, would you hire and train speakers for my new company on a stock market training? And we hit the ground running. I hired and trained 125 speakers. No one made less than $150,000 a year. And at that time, this would have been in our early nineties. That was a lot of money, even for speaker. They had no commitment, no nothing. Just I'd hired them. I trained them. And I had one rule that I would enforce if I was hiring you as a speaker. Have you ever paid $5,000 for any type of training? If their answer was no, I knew I can teach you how to speak, but if you have never gone through the experience of paying for information, how can you sell the idea of someone should pay for information? And I found that if they've never done it, I don't want them to speak. The obstacle is too hard for me to overcome convincing them all that. But if someone has said, yeah, I took every penny, every dollar in my wallet to get information I needed, when you told me that story a while ago, it resonated with me. That's exactly why you're good at this. Because you know what it feels like to give up everything in your wallet to get something that you know is going to make a difference to you. Yeah, these people, they use it anything. We took that stock market training and just plugged in the stock market content and around the chunks. And so it works anything. How many, how many chunks did you develop during your time? Are there more coming? Is there, is it finite? Is it, is there a certain number of them? Well, I never have counted them because a lot of it depends on you. You can't use them all in a 15 minute presentation. That's the reason I say you have to start with the end in mind. What is it you want to, what are the most difficult parts in your presentation that you found? Like a while ago, you said that people said, well, let me pay you out of the future. I have a chunk for that. But if the people don't know what objections are, you don't know which chunk to use. I have to be adaptable because you said something else. Want to go about the, the people that I had trained, the method of making money was to go out and do some type of split with the promoter, the people that get the people in a room. Well, those things were drying up. The internet changed it. Now the internet, this is a guy that's been in the business for years. And my dad, my son say, you're old dad. Well, because I've been here from the beginning, I can see the changes. And the first thing I would like to tell everybody is change will continue. It's not over yet. It's going to keep continuing with the big thing that's changed in our industry is that the internet now is where you speak. So if you don't know how to adapt to that, and then the other thing that I noticed is the internet is taken over. The people on the internet are way younger than me. My generation did not keep pace. The people who understand what works on the internet are the ones that are the top of the field today. And it's actually a much better profession. It's easier to get started because you don't have to worry about where am I going to get my speaking engagement? Do I have to get on an airplane? See, I live two and a half hours from an airport. Two and a from that. I have to be there an hour early. So if I go somewhere, I have to three and a half hours before my flight. I leave my house. Then I have to fly for an hour to make a 90 minute presentation and then fly home. That's not a lifestyle that I would promote. Now you get on zoom, you get on the internet, you make your entire presentation almost identical to if you were there. And then still people get enough run to the back of a room. You said, push the button down below and you'll get the result. It was promised to you. It's pretty much a summary of what's happening. The internet changed everything and it brought. It brought some sharp thinkers in that they have ideas and things that I never even dreamed about as I'm watching some of them. By the way, I like your book. I started reading that thing. Found somebody things like I do or maybe I'm copying you or whatever. It's awesome. And I'm thinking the younger guys are the ones that's going to carry this torch on in the future, but there's a blend here that will work. Everybody getting started. I used to start all my presentations off if I, if I've got time to do this. But telling stuff, this is called talking to the subconscious mind. I would say, now remember when I'm talking about starting with the end in mind, I was selling books and tapes. This is in the eighties. That's how we trans. That's how we delivered our information. And I said, well, by the way, I was, I was taking all my books and tapes out in the back of the room and the guy in charge of the convention comes up and hands me a broom. He says, sweep this stuff up. You don't understand. I'm one of the speakers here. And besides that, I'm a graduate of the university of Arkansas. He said, well, in that case, hand me the broom and I'll show you how to sweep it up. Well, I'll get a laugh out of that. And then I'll transcend into the next chunk. I said, but you know, that doesn't bother me anymore because I found out something. If you've done something successfully, if it's been time tested and proven that it works, I know that I could copy your system and get the very same results as a person that originated that. And I was amazed at this deep insight and success. And my wife says, well, I've been doing that for years. I said, what? She said, yeah, we'll go to a pot, a church potluck supper and she'll see this elaborate food dish. And she said, if I just had the recipe, what's she saying? If I had that written program that I could follow step by step, she would get the very same results as a person that originated that. Now, what I'm going to do in the next few minutes is I'm going to lay out a step by step program in which you can get the very same results as a person that originated that. Now, time out Russell. That's an introductory chunk, but you're talking to the subconscious mind. Now, what I mean by that, everybody in that audience knows number one, they know I'm married, yet I didn't have to bring that up. Number two, they know that I go to church. I did not have to bring that up. That's teaching to the subconscious mind. They also know that I have product for sale in the back of the room that I was sweeping up. They, I didn't say that I'm talking to their subconscious mind. They also know that anybody that has, if you have the recipe and that was validated by my wife, she said, but what my wife did more than what I did. I'm the man. I recognize the power if I had a proven system. She says, that's how she gets everything done. She has a recipe. It's validation. All of that is in a chunk and your people have my permission to use that, tweak it any way that form a faction. But that's what I mean by in the first part of your presentation, you need to know what you're saying has a purpose. And that purpose is going to take the people in your audience to your desired result at the end. Cool. I wish that we could like fast rewind back to 2009 before you're done to have everybody come to your event and go through all the stuff in depth and detail up is, it is so amazing. One of my closest friends, Stu McLaren, I know worked for you for a long time and and went to event after, you know, it was there in the middle of it. And you just like tell me the stories of all the stuff and all the stuff you guys were developing and learning. And it's just, it's so fun to hear from your, from your own mouth and to hear you talking about it. Man, I think for those who are, who are learners right now and they're trying to figure out like where, where can they find, how can they develop their own chunks? How can they find their own stories and things like that? Like what's the best way to think for people nowadays to really start figuring some of those things out themselves and their presentations? Well, I think the first thing starts with what I did back then is I sat down and wrote out a list of all the objections that people have brought up to go into my training, to buy in my product or buying the home study course or buying a webinar or whatever you're wanting to sell. Remember when I said start with the end in mind, where do you want to go? What do you want to sell? Maybe you're going to sell being coached, maybe you're going to sell mid mentor or whatever it is you start there. Now that you know where you're going to go, what are the objections that have come up before that have stopped people from participating? Each of those will become a chunk for you. So what you have to do is the way what I did in going through this, I took every objection and I told a story that overcame that objection before the objection ever comes up. And that's what children's chunks was all about is each one has a purpose. And I made them entertaining at least I thought they were my audience who tend to think you can make them funny, but you're teaching. Probably everyone should have in their presentation the thinking grow rich chunk. It's just critical that you learn that one because now you've got Napoleon Hill validating what your audience should do. Number one, set a goal. Number two, get specialized knowledge. What a unique opportunity that Napoleon Hill has just told them they need to have specialized knowledge and you're the first person in their lives to offer specialized knowledge. I don't want to go through that one too fast, but the thinking grow rich chunk should be in everybody's presentation because Napoleon Hill found out that there are over a dozen characteristics that all successful people have. But what I thought was the most important is this. If all successful people have these characteristics, then I want to develop them, but I thought the most important development was that no one was born with any of these successful characteristics. We all have to make a decision to acquire them. And that lack of decision is a decision or that decision is a decision. And I decided when I was younger, I will master those characteristics so that I can have the success that I want for my family. Everybody ought to have that in our presentation. So good. So good. I love this. Are we about five minutes left before our time's up? We talked earlier, you talked about in the first five minutes of your presentation, you share the chunk about, if you gave them $4, they give you $1 back. I love if you just kind of shared how, even if you just want to go back into script of doing it, because I think my favorite parts, very first time I've heard you speak, and it was like the most convincing sales argument I'd ever heard in my whole life. But you're talking about how your training was 20,000 or 50,000 or whatever. But you got everybody to pay you the 5,000 down and feel like we were getting a huge deal. We're like, Oh my gosh. Cause I think in the very beginning, at least for me, again, I was, I was a young 20, 21, 22 year old kid. I first heard this first presentation and you're $5,000. I was like, I've only made $5,000 total in my entire business to this day. And by the end, I thought it was like the best deal on planet earth. I was so excited. And you, and I, you convinced me that like, I was going to become a speaker and that was going to make money. And I was going to give you more money later. And it was going to be the greatest thing in the world. And I look at that. I'm like, how did you get a 22 year old Russell who had no money to feel like giving you $5,000? And then the fact that I was going to give you more money later was like the greatest deal on planet earth. Well, that all started with, uh, remember when I told you I was training 125 people, how to do stock market trainings? I went to the guy that owned the company. And I said, why are we giving this training away? Why are we not charging for it? He said, cause I don't want to charge for it. And I said, but if we, if we told these speakers, it's $25,000. However, if you don't, if we don't hire you, you don't have to pay it. But if we hired, we're going to hold it out of your paycheck until you get it paid off. Everyone would have paid that cause there's no risk still. He didn't want to do it. And I'm thinking, you know, it's such a powerful idea. Now this is kind of personal, but I want to share this with you. No one had ever offered an opportunity like that, but I felt like it would work. The first time I ever offered my speaker training, I was a master of ceremony at a Robert Allen event. And so I said, I'm going to do a speaker training. If you guys want to stay over a couple of days, it's 500 bucks. And that's about the extent of my clothes. 30 people signed up. Well, you can add up 30 people times 500 bucks is what I had. Then, but I had to split that with Robert Allen's company. Well, the next time we offered that, I said, I'm going to be doing a speaker training. It's $1,500. And if you want to stay a couple of, I had about 30 people signed up. I'm thinking, you know, there's a certain percentage that are interested in this product, but evidently the price is no object. My mom died many years ago, about 20, not forget how many years ago, but I went to her funeral in the morning. And that afternoon I had to speak in Orlando, Florida. And let me tell you the setup. You did what's called breakout rooms. Breakout rooms means all the people in the event are divided up and they go to a smaller room to listen to what would be a lesser speaker. The main speakers are in the main platform where everybody has to listen to them, but the breakout room is not a, you're not going to sell a lot there. And it was the last breakout room of the entire four day event. So everybody has spent all their money. You got all these reasons. So I showed up and I did several things at that event that had never happened before. Ever, but all the staff was gone because they're packing up. So I had to use my contract clothes because no one was going to be able to do anything. But I made a change. I said, I am going to do a speaker training is $15,000. And I know you may think that's a large amount of money, but I am so confident that it will work for you. You give me $3,000 now and I will finance the rest of it. And that was the first time I ever did it, but I did something else. I looked at them and I said, now it's not something that I'm just going to teach you. It's a program that you're going to emerge yourself into. I'm going to tell you how to do it. I'm going to show you how to do it. And then you're going to do it. And do I have your permission to push you to make you do, because if you don't do what I tell you to do, you won't get it. That's the way my program works. Now, let me tell you something. You may make a small deposit right now, but you owe me a thousands of dollars. And I want you to look me in eye. I'm going to get my money. And I would tell you, how many of you liked the idea that I'm going to get my money? Now, tie that into a fact that they only give me a dollar after I've given them the four looking them in an eye. That became what I wanted. Look in them in an eye. That became what Alex Mondojan said, John, that is your trademark phrase. I'm going to get my money. And I'll ask you, I'll ask my audience, how many of you want me to get my money? And everybody raises their hand because it's a different approach to selling information. It's the greatest. I remember that to this day. I remember you're like, the five thousand dollars right now, but you stole me twenty thousand dollars. You have my money. And I'm going to get my money. And I remember you say to me, oh my gosh, I'm going to go on stage. I'm going to be speaking and then 15,000 from you. I make a million. It'll be like nothing. It'll be so easy. I was just like, so, so God, I'm thinking about actually it's funny and I'm working on a new presentation for hiring and coaching program we have. And I did the first presentation and it, it, you know, that feeling you wanted it. Just like it worked. People are jumping over the table. I was like, it's over. Like this is going to be the biggest thing we've ever done. And you know, we've grown ClickFunnels to over a hundred million dollars this year alone. And it was all on the back of a presentation. We crafted and I liken it to like, we could have gone and got venture capital. And I look at our competitors who, you know, one took 40 million, one took like 170 million and instead we had one presentation, one funnel that, that was the equivalent of that. And I feel like I could, I could use that same presentation, going back to people and saying, look, um, I could be your, I could finance it. I could be your venture capital partner.
